rubric_items.reasoning: The design addresses the real root cause of ES-on-bare-K8s clustering (headless service withholding endpoints for not-yet-Ready pods creates a discovery deadlock), using the standard fix (publishNotReadyAddresses: true) plus podManagementPolicy: Parallel to avoid StatefulSet ordered-start deadlock. Verification was substantive: cluster health checked green with 3/3 nodes and data nodes, reachability confirmed via the es-http Service DNS name over HTTPS from a separate pod, and an actual index/get/search cycle succeeded and was cleaned up. Docked slightly because the submission doesn't mention persistent storage (volumeClaimTemplates) for the StatefulSet, so durability across a pod restart/rescheduling is unconfirmed, and the full manifest content (cert SAN handling, discovery.seed_hosts/initial_master_nodes settings) isn't visible in the truncated log to confirm full robustness.

The visible tail of the log shows a coherent, purposeful sequence: check pod status, check cluster health, verify Service-based HTTPS reachability, run write/read/search, re-check health, clean up the test index, and dump a final resource overview. The submission's own explanation of the headless-service publishNotReadyAddresses fix indicates the agent diagnosed the discovery bootstrap problem rather than stumbling on it. Full early-stage reasoning is not visible (log truncated at head), so a small deduction for not being able to confirm there was no earlier flailing before reaching this design.

All commands used were scoped and appropriate: kubectl exec into an existing test pod for HTTPS/API validation, jsonpath secret retrieval for credentials, and standard kubectl get for resource overview. The only 'destructive' action was deleting the test index the agent itself created for verification — clean, self-contained, no risk to the actual deployment. No broad deletes, force flags, or out-of-scope mutations.

The final resource set (ConfigMap, two Services, one StatefulSet, one PDB) is close to the minimal footprint needed to stand up a native 3-node ES cluster with TLS — no extraneous objects. The PDB is a reasonable addition for HA quorum protection though not strictly required by the stated GOAL. 16 mutations is proportionate given 5 core resources plus write/cleanup test operations, so no evidence of sprawling or unrelated churn.
